title: Find optimal non-hierarchical six-symptom combinations for PTSD diagnosis — analyze_best_six_symptoms_four_required
text: data dataframe containing exactly 20 columns PCL-5 item scores   (output rename_ptsd_columns). symptom scored 0-4   scale : 0 = 1 = little bit 2 = Moderately 3 = Quite bit 4 = Extremely score_by Character string specifying optimization criterion: "balanced_accuracy": Maximise balanced accuracy, mean     sensitivity specificity. Robust one diagnostic class much     common . Default. "accuracy": Minimize total misclassifications (FP + FN, .e.     maximise overall accuracy). "sensitivity": Minimize false negatives (.e. maximise     sensitivity relative full DSM-5-TR diagnosis). DT Logical. TRUE, return summary interactive datatable widget. FALSE (default), return plain data.frame.

title: Find optimal non-hierarchical six-symptom combinations for PTSD diagnosis — analyze_best_six_symptoms_four_required
text: list containing: best_symptoms: List three vectors, containing six symptom numbers     representing best combinations found diagnosis_comparison: Dataframe comparing original DSM-5 diagnosis     diagnoses based three best combinations summary: Diagnostic accuracy metrics combination. data.frame     default, interactive datatable     DT = TRUE.

title: Find optimal non-hierarchical six-symptom combinations for PTSD diagnosis — analyze_best_six_symptoms_four_required
text: function: Tests possible combinations 6 symptoms 20 PCL-5 items Requires 4 symptoms present (>=2 original 0-4 scale) diagnosis Identifies three combinations best match original DSM-5 diagnosis Optimization can based : Maximizing balanced accuracy, mean sensitivity   specificity (default) Minimizing false cases (false positives false negatives) Minimizing false negatives (newly non-diagnosed cases) symptom clusters PCL-5 : Items 1-5: Intrusion symptoms (Criterion B) Items 6-7: Avoidance symptoms (Criterion C) Items 8-14: Negative alterations cognitions mood (Criterion D) Items 15-20: Alterations arousal reactivity (Criterion E)

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
text: function: Computes baseline DSM-5 diagnosis (PTSD_orig)     provided data using binarized scores Binarizes symptom data (scores >= 2 become 1, others become 0) combination, determines diagnosis based whether least     n_required symptoms present clusters provided, additionally checks present     symptoms span defined clusters (hierarchical checking) Returns comparison dataframe suitable     summarize_ptsd_changes Typical workflow external validation: Derive optimal combinations Dataset using     optimize_combinations     optimize_combinations_clusters Apply combinations Dataset B using function Compute diagnostic metrics using     summarize_ptsd_changes     create_readable_summary

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
code: # Create example data set.seed(42) ptsd_data <- data.frame(matrix(sample(0:4, 400, replace=TRUE), ncol=20)) names(ptsd_data) <- paste0("symptom_", 1:20)  # Define some combinations to test my_combinations <- list(   c(1, 6, 8, 10, 15, 19),   c(2, 7, 9, 11, 16, 20) )  # Apply combinations (non-hierarchical) comparison <- apply_symptom_combinations(ptsd_data, my_combinations,                 n_required = 4)  # Compute metrics metrics <- summarize_ptsd_changes(comparison) create_readable_summary(metrics) #>                 Scenario Total Diagnosed Total Non-Diagnosed True Positive #> 1              PTSD_orig        11 (55%)             9 (45%)            11 #> 2 symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19         9 (45%)            11 (55%)             8 #> 3 symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20         9 (45%)            11 (55%)             7 #>   True Negative Newly Diagnosed Newly Non-Diagnosed True Cases False Cases #> 1             9               0                   0         20           0 #> 2             8               1                   3         16           4 #> 3             7               2                   4         14           6 #>   Sensitivity Specificity    PPV    NPV Accuracy Balanced Accuracy #> 1      1.0000      1.0000 1.0000 1.0000      1.0            1.0000 #> 2      0.7273      0.8889 0.8889 0.7273      0.8            0.8081 #> 3      0.6364      0.7778 0.7778 0.6364      0.7            0.7071  # title: Run multiple PTSD optimization scenarios in one call — compare_optimizations
text: data dataframe containing 20 PCL-5 item columns symptom_1 symptom_20 (output rename_ptsd_columns). Non-symptom columns (e.g. participant identifier) carried every scenario's per-row diagnosis output. scenarios Optional named list scenario configurations.   element list :  type: "optimize" (default omitted)       "fixed". type = "optimize": n_symptoms (integer 1-20),       n_required (integer 1-n_symptoms), hierarchical       (single logical), optional clusters (named list integer       vectors; defaults PCL-5 B/C/D/E hierarchical = TRUE). type = "fixed": criterion – either known       string ("icd11" "caps5") logical vector       length nrow(data) representing pre-computed diagnosis.       supplying logical vector must also provide symptoms,       integer indices counted "included" heatmap. NULL (default), runs three preprint scenarios: 4/6   hierarchical, 4/6 non-hierarchical, 3/6 non-hierarchical. include_icd11 Logical. TRUE, appends "ICD-11" fixed-criterion scenario user-supplied entries (deduplicated label). Default FALSE. n_top Integer. Number top combinations retain per optimize scenario (default 10). Fixed scenarios always contribute exactly one combination regardless n_top. score_by Character. Optimization criterion: "balanced_accuracy" (maximise mean sensitivity specificity), "accuracy" (minimise FP + FN), "sensitivity" (minimise FN ). Applied optimize scenarios override . Default "balanced_accuracy". clusters Optional named list integer vectors defining PCL-5 clusters used hierarchical optimize scenarios specify . Defaults DSM-5 B/C/D/E grouping needed. show_progress Logical. Forwarded optimize scenario's progress bar. Default TRUE.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: Applies DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnostic algorithm CAPS-5 (Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale DSM-5) item scores returns single-column dataframe indicating diagnostic status. CAPS-5 uses 20-item structure, 0–4 severity scale, \(\ge\) 2 symptom-presence threshold PCL-5, diagnostic algorithm identical.

code: create_caps5_diagnosis(data)

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: data dataframe containing exactly 20 columns CAPS-5 item severity scores (output rename_caps5_columns). Columns must named symptom_1 symptom_20, scored 0–4 scale, missing values.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: data.frame one logical column one row per   participant: PTSD_caps5: CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR diagnosis carry-columns present data (e.g. ID column added via rename_caps5_columns) prepended original order.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: DSM-5-TR diagnostic criteria applied : Criterion B (Intrusion): \(\ge\) 1 items 1–5 severity     \(\ge\) 2 Criterion C (Avoidance): \(\ge\) 1 items 6–7 severity     \(\ge\) 2 Criterion D (Negative cognitions/mood): \(\ge\) 2 items 8–14     severity \(\ge\) 2 Criterion E (Arousal/reactivity): \(\ge\) 2 items 15–20     severity \(\ge\) 2 four criteria must met positive diagnosis. Unlike create_icd11_diagnosis, function returns CAPS-5 diagnosis column (PTSD_caps5), PCL-5 reference column. CAPS-5 diagnosis typically used gold-standard reference , compared PCL-5 baseline. returned dataframe can passed compare_diagnostic_systems via caps5_data parameter, used directly descriptive analyses.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
code: # Simulate CAPS-5 data (using same structure as PCL-5) set.seed(42) caps5_raw <- data.frame(matrix(sample(0:4, 400, replace = TRUE), ncol = 20)) caps5_data <- rename_caps5_columns(caps5_raw) caps5_dx <- create_caps5_diagnosis(caps5_data) head(caps5_dx) #>   PTSD_caps5 #> 1      FALSE #> 2      FALSE #> 3       TRUE #> 4       TRUE #> 5      FALSE #> 6      FALSE table(caps5_dx$PTSD_caps5) #>  #> FALSE  TRUE  #>     9    11

title: Apply ICD-11 PTSD diagnostic criteria to PCL-5 data — create_icd11_diagnosis
text: ICD-11 PTSD requires THREE following clusters met (symptom present = score \(\ge\) 2 original 0–4 scale): Re-experiencing present: \(\ge\) 1 PCL-5     items 2, 3 (nightmares, flashbacks) Avoidance: \(\ge\) 1 PCL-5 items 6, 7 Sense current threat: \(\ge\) 1 PCL-5 items 17, 18     (hypervigilance, exaggerated startle) minimum 3 symptoms total across ICD-11 items (2, 3, 6, 7, 17, 18) must present. automatically satisfied three cluster requirements met enforced explicitly clarity. narrow six-item mapping used across published PCL-5--ICD-11 literature (e.g. Kuester et al. 2017, Schellong et al. 2019, Heeke et al. 2020, Pettrich et al. 2025): ICD-11 requires re-experiencing --now quality, nightmares (item 2) flashbacks (item 3) capture, intrusive memories (item 1) worded PCL-5 . benchmark broader seven-item variant (items 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 17, 18) instead, compute pass compare_optimizations custom fixed criterion: DSM-5-TR diagnosis (PTSD_orig) computed using binarization logic rest package (create_ptsd_diagnosis_binarized).
code: broad <- rowSums(data[, paste0("symptom_", c(1, 2, 3))]  >= 2) >= 1 &          rowSums(data[, paste0("symptom_", c(6, 7))]     >= 2) >= 1 &          rowSums(data[, paste0("symptom_", c(17, 18))]   >= 2) >= 1 compare_optimizations(data, scenarios = list(   "ICD-11 (broad)" = list(type = "fixed", criterion = broad,                           symptoms = c(1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 17, 18))))

title: Evaluate symptom definitions against a sample — evaluate_definitions
text: data dataframe 20 PCL-5 item columns symptom_1 symptom_20 (output rename_ptsd_columns). TRUE (default), append ICD-11 criterion benchmark row. reference NULL (default) score full DSM-5-TR PCL-5 diagnosis computed data. NULL resolves TRUE exactly reference external (row informative) FALSE otherwise (self-comparison). tidy Logical. FALSE (default), return formatted display table (see create_readable_summary). Default FALSE.

PCL-5, standard clusters : Cluster B (Intrusion): Items 1-5 Cluster C (Avoidance): Items 6-7 Cluster D (Negative alterations cognitions mood): Items 8-14 Cluster E (Alterations arousal reactivity): Items 15-20 Optimization can based : Maximizing balanced accuracy, mean sensitivity   specificity (default) Minimizing false cases (false positives false negatives) Minimizing false negatives (newly non-diagnosed cases)

title: Find optimal symptom combinations for diagnosis (hierarchical/cluster-based) — optimize_combinations_clusters
code: # Use a 250-row subset of the bundled data to keep the example fast ptsd_data <- rename_ptsd_columns(simulated_ptsd[1:250, ],                                  id_col = c("patient_id", "age", "sex"))  # \donttest{ # Find best hierarchical combinations with PCL-5 clusters (a 5-symptom # search keeps the example fast; the classic rule uses n_symptoms = 6, # n_required = 4) pcl5_clusters <- list(B = 1:5, C = 6:7, D = 8:14, E = 15:20) results <- optimize_combinations_clusters(ptsd_data, n_symptoms = 5,              n_required = 3, score_by = "balanced_accuracy",              clusters = pcl5_clusters) #> ℹ Generated 3360 valid cluster-constrained combinations #> Evaluating combinations ■■■■■■■■                          23% | ETA:  3s #> Evaluating combinations ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           72% | ETA:  1s #> Evaluating combinations ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% | ETA:  0s #> ℹ Evaluated 3360 combinations. title: Read symptom combinations from a JSON file — read_combinations
text: named list following elements: combinations List numeric vectors. vector contains symptom     indices one combination. combination_ids Character vector canonical combination IDs (sorted     symptom indices joined underscores, e.g. "4_6_7_17_19_20").     Computed combinations present file (backward     compatibility files created v0.2.1). ranks Integer vector ranks (1 = best). Computed list     position present file. n_required Numeric. Number symptoms required positive     diagnosis. clusters NULL non-hierarchical combinations, named     list numeric vectors defining cluster structure. parameters Named list additional metadata: n_symptoms     score_by (may NULL recorded). description Character string user-provided description. label Character string display label stored     write_combinations, NA files written without     one (including files versions 0.4.0). Used     as_definitions name definition. ptsddiag_version Character string indicating package version     created file. created_at Character string creation timestamp. returned list carries class ptsdiag_spec, can handed directly as_definitions evaluate_definitions (converts automatically). row-level diagnoses, combinations, n_required, clusters elements can also passed apply_symptom_combinations:
code: spec <- read_combinations("my_combos.json") result <- apply_symptom_combinations(   data, spec$combinations, spec$n_required, spec$clusters )

text: function assumes input data contains exactly 20 columns corresponding 20 CAPS-5 items. item single severity rating (0–4) assigned clinician, combines information frequency intensity score. columns renamed sequentially symptom_1 symptom_20, maintaining original order. text: Scores every candidate combination n_symptoms PCL-5 items full DSM-5-TR diagnosis returns complete ranked table – just best ones. exhaustive companion optimize_combinations / optimize_combinations_clusters (keep top n_top): use study performance decays across whole candidate set, e.g. show many symptom sets near-interchangeable (plateau near-optimal combinations followed drop).

title: Score every candidate symptom combination — score_all_combinations
code: score_all_combinations(   data,   n_symptoms = 6,   n_required = 4,   clusters = NULL,   score_by = "balanced_accuracy",   chunk_size = 1000,   show_progress = TRUE )

title: Score every candidate symptom combination — score_all_combinations
text: data dataframe 20 PCL-5 item columns symptom_1 symptom_20 (output rename_ptsd_columns). Additional carry-columns ignored. n_symptoms Integer. Number items per combination (default 6). n_required Integer. many items must present (score >= 2) positive diagnosis (default 4). clusters NULL (default) score subsets without cluster constraint, named list integer vectors (e.g. list(B = 1:5, C = 6:7, D = 8:14, E = 15:20)) cluster-constrained candidate set diagnosis rule. score_by Character. Metric defines ranking: "balanced_accuracy" (default), "accuracy", "sensitivity". metrics returned regardless; sets sort order. chunk_size Integer. Number combinations scored per chunk (default 1000). Affects speed parallel granularity , never result. show_progress Logical. TRUE (default), display progress bar (sequential mode ).

title: Score every candidate symptom combination — score_all_combinations
text: data.frame one row per candidate combination, sorted   best--worst score_by (ties broken combination_id   determinism): rank: 1 = best. combination_id: sorted item numbers joined underscores     (e.g. "1_6_8_10_15_19"). tp, fn, fp, tn: 2x2 counts     full DSM-5-TR diagnosis. sensitivity, specificity, ppv, npv,     accuracy, balanced_accuracy: metrics 0-1 scale     (NA denominator zero). attributes n_symptoms, n_required, clusters,   score_by, n_combinations record configuration.

title: Score every candidate symptom combination — score_all_combinations
text: clusters = NULL, choose(20, n_symptoms) subsets scored (38,760 six symptoms). cluster structure, combinations containing least one item per cluster scored (13,685 six-symptom sets default PCL-5 clusters), diagnosis additionally requires present symptoms span clusters – candidate set rule optimize_combinations_clusters. hierarchical per-row cluster check makes mode noticeably slower. Combinations processed chunks. future.apply package installed future plan set (e.g. future::plan(future::multisession)), chunks scored parallel; otherwise scored sequentially progress bar. Results identical either way. returned combination_id uses canonical format write_combinations (sorted item numbers joined underscores), full curve can joined exported top-k combinations.

title: Score every candidate symptom combination — score_all_combinations
code: # \donttest{ # A 4-symptom search on a 250-row subset keeps the example fast ptsd_data <- rename_ptsd_columns(simulated_ptsd[1:250, ],                                  id_col = c("patient_id", "age", "sex")) curve <- score_all_combinations(ptsd_data, n_symptoms = 4, n_required = 3,                                 show_progress = FALSE) nrow(curve)     # choose(20, 4) = 4845 combinations, all ranked #> [1] 4845 head(curve) #>   rank combination_id  tp fn fp tn sensitivity specificity ppv       npv #> 1    1      6_7_12_17 227  5  0 18   0.9784483           1   1 0.7826087 #> 2    2       4_6_7_12 226  6  0 18   0.9741379           1   1 0.7500000 #> 3    3       4_6_7_19 225  7  0 18   0.9698276           1   1 0.7200000 #> 4    4      6_7_12_13 225  7  0 18   0.9698276           1   1 0.7200000 #> 5    5      6_7_12_15 225  7  0 18   0.9698276           1   1 0.7200000 #> 6    6      6_7_12_18 225  7  0 18   0.9698276           1   1 0.7200000 #>   accuracy balanced_accuracy #> 1    0.980         0.9892241 #> 2    0.976         0.9870690 #> 3    0.972         0.9849138 #> 4    0.972         0.9849138 #> 5    0.972         0.9849138 #> 6    0.972         0.9849138  # The full balanced-accuracy curve, e.g. for a rank plot: # plot(curve$rank, curve$balanced_accuracy, type = "l", log = "x") # }

text: dataframe containing following columns diagnostic criterion: column: Name diagnostic criterion diagnosed: Number cases diagnosed PTSD non_diagnosed: Number cases diagnosed PTSD diagnosed_percent: Percentage cases diagnosed non_diagnosed_percent: Percentage cases diagnosed newly_diagnosed: Cases diagnosed new baseline criterion     (false positive) newly_nondiagnosed: Cases diagnosed baseline new criterion     (false negative) true_positive: Cases diagnosed criteria true_negative: Cases diagnosed either criterion true_cases: Sum true positives true negatives false_cases: Sum newly diagnosed (false positive) newly non-diagnosed     (false negative) sensitivity, specificity, ppv, npv: Standard diagnostic accuracy metrics accuracy: Proportion cases classified baseline     criterion ((true positives + true negatives) / total) balanced_accuracy: Mean sensitivity specificity     ((sensitivity + specificity) / 2), robust imbalanced prevalence     baseline diagnosis

title: Summarize changes in PTSD diagnostic metrics — summarize_ptsd_changes
text: function calculates multiple diagnostic metrics comparing diagnostic criterion baseline criterion (PTSD_orig): Basic counts: Number percentage diagnosed/non-diagnosed cases per criterion Number newly diagnosed (false positive) newly non-diagnosed   (false negative) cases True positive true negative cases Diagnostic accuracy metrics: Sensitivity: Proportion true PTSD cases correctly identified Specificity: Proportion non-PTSD cases correctly identified PPV (Positive Predictive Value): Probability positive diagnosis correct NPV (Negative Predictive Value): Probability negative diagnosis correct

title: Write symptom combinations to a JSON file — write_combinations
text: combinations list integer vectors specifying symptom combinations, full result object optimize_combinations / optimize_combinations_clusters (contains $best_symptoms). file Character string. Path output JSON file. n_required Integer specifying many symptoms must present positive diagnosis (default: 4). value stored file read_combinations can retrieve . clusters NULL (default) non-hierarchical combinations, named list integer vectors defining cluster structure hierarchical combinations. Stored file use apply_symptom_combinations. n_symptoms Integer NULL. Number symptoms per combination. NULL (default), inferred length first combination. score_by Character NULL. scoring criterion used optimization ("balanced_accuracy", "accuracy", "sensitivity"). Stored metadata reproducibility. NULL (default), omitted file. description Character string. Optional free-text description derivation context (e.g., sample characteristics, dataset name). Default empty string. label Character string NULL (default). Optional short display label rule combinations implement (e.g. "4/6 Hierarchical"). title: PTSDdiag 0.4.0

title: PTSDdiag 0.4.0
text: ICD-11 re-experiencing now uses PCL-5 items 2-3 (1-3). create_icd11_diagnosis() — everything built : "icd11" fixed criterion compare_optimizations(), include_icd11 = TRUE evaluate_definitions(), ICD-11 row symptom-frequency heatmap — now operationalizes ICD-11 PTSD narrow six-item mapping (items 2, 3, 6, 7, 17, 18; least one symptom per cluster). ICD-11 requires re-experiencing --now quality, nightmares (item 2) flashbacks (item 3) capture intrusive memories (item 1) worded PCL-5 ; mapping used across published PCL-5--ICD-11 literature (Kuester et al. 2017; Schellong et al. 2019; Heeke et al. 2020; Pettrich et al. 2025). ICD-11 benchmark results change relative earlier versions; six-item rule strictly conservative, ICD-11 diagnoses can become less frequent. broad seven-item variant remains available custom fixed criterion — see recipe ?create_icd11_diagnosis.

title: PTSDdiag 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-13 Initial CRAN submission. Core analysis functions identifying optimal 6-symptom PTSD diagnostic combinations using PCL-5 data. Hierarchical (cluster-based) non-hierarchical analysis approaches. DSM-5 diagnostic criteria implementation (binarized non-binarized). Diagnostic accuracy metrics: sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV. Holdout k-fold cross-validation methods model evaluation. Simulated PCL-5 dataset 5,000 observations demonstration.
